description_long: This is a traditional Japanese katana with a gently curved, single-edged steel blade and a distinct hamon (temper line) visible along the edge. The handle is wrapped in a brown cord, likely over ray skin, and features a gold-colored pommel and a circular hand guard (tsuba) with subtle decorative elements. The katana is paired with a black lacquered wooden scabbard (saya) adorned with gold and silver accents, including a sageo cord for securing the sword. The fittings on the scabbard and handle are metallic, with some engraved or embossed patterns. The overall craftsmanship suggests a blend of functionality and artistry, typical of high-quality swords. The object is slender and elongated, with a balance between the blade and the handle for effective use.
description: A traditional Japanese katana with a curved steel blade, brown cord-wrapped handle, and a black lacquered scabbard with gold and silver accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved blade, the brown-wrapped handle, and the detailed black scabbard with gold accents.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow profile of the blade and scabbard, with the handle and sageo cord visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's curvature, the handle's wrapping, and the scabbard's decorative fittings.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the end of the handle and the scabbard tip, showing the pommel and cord attachment.
attribute_blade_type: curved, single-edged katana blade with visible hamon
attribute_handle_design: brown cord-wrapped, cylindrical, with gold pommel and ergonomic grip
attribute_ornamentation: gold and silver accents, engraved fittings, decorative tsuba
